explanation: This structured natural language property captures the main intent of the requirement: in PSV Mode, while the controller is in the expiration phase, within the specific time interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], and if no spontaneous breath is detected (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), if an Expiratory Pause is requested by the user (via expPauseButton), then an Expiratory Pause shall be started. The property makes use of the available atomic propositions to describe the state conditions (PSVMode, expiratoryPhase, expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag, dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV, expPauseButton, expiratoryPause). It avoids any numerical expressions and directly expresses the captured dependency.
decision1_substring: If the controller is in expiration phase and a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ) is false), within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag]
decision2_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
decision3_substring: an Expiratory Pause shall start (see CONT.42).
output_structured_natural_language: while PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV, upon expiratoryPauseButton, immediately satisfy expiratoryPause

explanation: The natural language requirement specifies that during the expiration phase in PSV mode, when no spontaneous breath is detected (that is, dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), and within a specific timer window ([min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], represented here by expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag), if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then an Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPause) must be started. To capture this, we use the structured template: "while bool_exp1, whenever bool_exp2, immediately satisfy bool_exp3", where bool_exp1 encapsulates being in "expiration phase AND PSV mode AND within timer window AND no spontaneous breath detected", bool_exp2 is "expiratoryPauseButton", and bool_exp3 is "expiratoryPause". This ensures the Expiratory Pause only happens under the required conjunction of conditions and responds appropriately to the GUI input.
decision1_substring: controller is in expiration phase and a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ) is false), within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag]
decision2_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
decision3_substring: an Expiratory Pause shall start (see CONT.42)
output_structured_natural_language: while expiratoryPhase & PSVMode & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV, whenever expiratoryPauseButton, immediately satisfy expiratoryPause

explanation:
This translation breaks the input requirement into logical, structured steps:
1. The context is within PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode during the expiratory phase.
2. It only applies when a spontaneous breath is NOT detected, which, according to the atomic propositions, is when 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' is false (negated).
3. The time window is specified as the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], which can be mapped to 'expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag'.
4. The trigger for action is the setting of Expiratory Pause by the GUI ('expiratoryPauseButton').
5. The required action is to initiate Expiratory Pause ('expiratoryPause').
The structured NL replicates this by specifying that, whenever the relevant conditions in PSV mode, during expiration, without spontaneous breath, and within the interval, if the button is set, then eventually the expiratory pause must be activated.
decision1_substring: the controller is in expiration phase and a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ) is false), within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag]
decision2_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
decision3_substring: an Expiratory Pause shall start (see CONT.42).
output_structured_natural_language: while PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag, whenever expiratoryPauseButton, eventually satisfy expiratoryPause

These conditions are formalized as: 'whenever' the conjunction of these four factors, 'upon' expiratoryPauseButton, 'immediately' require expiratoryPause to hold. This accurately mirrors the described trigger-action relationship in the NL requirement.

decision2_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
decision3_substring: an Expiratory Pause shall start (see CONT.42)

The substrings used for each decision are selected corresponding to their referenced requirement in the sentence.

decision2_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
decision3_substring: an Expiratory Pause shall start (see CONT.42)
